description:
This image depicts a network topology diagram featuring two Ubuntu servers (`ubuntu1` and `ubuntu2`) connected to two Cisco switches (`cisco1` and `cisco2`). The Ubuntu hosts have interfaces labeled `ens2 (eth1)` with IP addresses `10.10.10.1/24` and `30.30.30.1/24`, respectively, while the Cisco switches use interfaces `E0/2` and `E0/1` for connectivity. The diagram shows a linear connection flow: `ubuntu1` → `cisco1` (E0/2) → `cisco2` (E0/1) → `ubuntu2`. All devices are labeled with their hostnames, interface names, and IP addresses. No errors or anomalies are visible in the topology.

**Search keywords for retrieval:**
- Network topology diagram
- Cisco switch configuration
- Ubuntu server networking
- Layer 2 connectivity
- IP addressing (10.10.10.x/24, 30.30.30.x/24)
- Draw.io network diagram
